claimText: Stablecoins are a type of cryptocurrency whose value is pegged to another currency, commodity, or financial instrument to maintain stability.
evidenceExcerpt: Stablecoins are pegged to currencies, commodities, or financial instruments to maintain value.
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition and purpose of stablecoins.
distractorHooks: Stablecoins are completely unpegged and highly volatile
Stablecoins are issued exclusively by central banks

claimText: The three primary types of stablecoins are fiat-collateralized, crypto-collateralized, and non-collateralized (algorithmic).
evidenceExcerpt: There are three primary types of stablecoins: fiat-collateralized, crypto-collateralized, and non-collateralized (algorithmic).
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying valid types of stablecoins.
distractorHooks: Algorithmic stablecoins are backed by physical gold

claimText: The Finance Act of 2022 imposed a 30% tax on capital gains arising from Virtual Digital Assets (VDAs) in India.
evidenceExcerpt: India imposed a 30% tax on capital gains from Virtual Digital Assets (VDAs) via the Finance Act, 2022.
examUse: Statement-based question on the taxation and legal status of cryptocurrencies in India.
distractorHooks: VDAs are completely tax-exempt in India
The tax rate on VDAs is 10%

claimText: Virtual Digital Assets (VDAs) were brought under the purview of the Prevention of Money-laundering Act (PMLA), 2002, in the year 2023.
evidenceExcerpt: In 2023, VDAs were brought under the purview of the Prevention and Money-laundering Act, 2002.
examUse: Statement-based question regarding the regulatory framework and anti-money laundering compliance for crypto assets in India.
distractorHooks: VDAs are exempt from the Prevention of Money-laundering Act
VDAs are regulated by the RBI under the FEMA Act instead of PMLA

claimText: Cryptocurrencies are based on blockchain technology, which functions as a distributed public ledger, and are typically created through a process called mining.
evidenceExcerpt: Cryptocurrency is based on blockchain, a distributed public ledger, and created via mining.
examUse: Statement-based question on the underlying technology of cryptocurrencies.
distractorHooks: Blockchain is a centralized private ledger controlled by a single entity
Cryptocurrencies are printed by central banks
